ddls:
CREATE TABLE "security_levels" (
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the security level (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the security level */,
  "min_password_length" INTEGER /* Minimum password length required for this security level */,
  "max_password_length" INTEGER /* Maximum password length allowed for this security level */,
  "password_strength" TEXT /* Password strength required for this security level (e.g., weak, fair, strong) */,
  "case_sensitive" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if passwords must be case-sensitive for this security level */,
  "non_alphabetical_required" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if non-alphabetical characters are required for this security level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password policy */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the security level the policy applies to */,
  "key_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy key (e.g., minPasswordLength, caseSensitivePassword) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the policy (e.g., 6+, Yes, No) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_policies_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES security_levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., standard_user, admin, external_partner) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  "default_security_level" INTEGER /* Default security level assigned to this role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_security_levels" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user role */,
  "level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the security level the role is assigned to */,
  "override_default" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if this security level overrides the default security level for the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_role_security_levels_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("level_id") REFERENCES security_levels ("level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user role */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed value of the user's password */,
  "password_salt" TEXT /* Salt used in the password hashing process */,
  "last_password_change" TEXT /* Date the user last changed their password */,
  "password_expiration" TEXT /* Date when the user's password will expire */,
  "mfa_enabled" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if multi-factor authentication is enabled for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password history entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed value of the user's password */,
  "password_salt" TEXT /* Salt used in the password hashing process */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date the password was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_attempts" (
  "attempt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password attempt */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "attempt_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the password attempt */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the attempt was made */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the attempt was successful */,
  "mfa_used" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if multi-factor authentication was used for this attempt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attempt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_attempts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., password_expiration, policy_change) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "read_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was read by the user */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date the notification will expire */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., password_change, policy_update) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the action */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the action */,
  "affected_role" INTEGER /* Reference to the affected role (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_education" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each educational resource */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource (e.g., guide, video, article) */,
  "resource_url" TEXT /* URL to the resource */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the resource */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource (e.g., video, article, guide) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_resources" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the educational resource */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the resource was accessed */,
  "completed" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user has completed the resource */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_resources_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES user_education ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_resources_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mfa_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each multi-factor authentication method */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the multi-factor authentication method (e.g., SMS, authenticator app) */,
  "method_description" TEXT /* Description of the multi-factor authentication method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mfa_methods_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
